title: Ensure Image Vulnerability Scanning using Microsoft  Defender for Cloud (MDC) image scanning or a third party  provider

input_row.title: Ensure Image Vulnerability Scanning using Microsoft  Defender for Cloud (MDC) image scanning or a third party  provider
input_row.description: Scan images being deployed to Azure (AKS) for vulnerabilities. Vulnerability scanning for images stored in Microsoft Defender for Cloud (MDC). This capability is powered by Microsoft Defender for Endpoint's MDVM, a leading provider of information security. When you push an image to Container Registry, MDC automatically scans it, then checks for known vulnerabilities in packages or dependencies defined in the file. When the scan completes (after about 10 minutes), MDC provides details and a security classification for each vulnerability detected, along with guidance on how to remediate issues and protect vulnerable attack surfaces.
input_row.rationale: Vulnerabilities in software packages can be exploited by hackers or malicious users to obtain unauthorized access to local cloud resources.
input_row.audit: Check MDC for Container Registries: This command shows whether container registries is enabled, which includes the image scanning feature. az security pricing show --name ContainerRegistry or az security pricing list --query "[ type=='Microsoft.ContainerRegistry/registries'].{Name:name, Status:properties.status}" -o table Internal Only - General
input_row.remediation: Enable MDC for Container Registries: If you find that container registries is not enabled and you wish to enable it, you can do so using the following command: az security pricing create --name ContainerRegistry --tier Standard or az resource update --ids /subscriptions/{subscription- id}/resourceGroups/{resource-group- name}/providers/Microsoft.ContainerRegistry/registries/{registry-name} --set properties.enabled=true replacing subscription-id, resource-group-name and registry-name with the appropriate values. Please note, enabling MDC for container registries incurs additional costs, so be sure to review the pricing details on the official Azure documentation before enabling it.
input_row.impact: When using an MDC, you might occasionally encounter problems. For example, you might not be able to pull a container image because of an issue with Docker in your local environment. Or, a network issue might prevent you from connecting to the registry.
input_row.default_value: Images are not scanned by Default.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ecr_repository_image_vulnerability_scanning_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API call/boto3 methods to use:**
  - Use `boto3` ECR client to check the image scanning configuration:
    - `ecr.describe_repositories(repositoryNames=[<repository-name>])` to get the repository settings.
- **AWS CLI commands:**
  - `aws ecr describe-repositories --repository-names <repository-name>` to retrieve repository settings directly.
- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - ECR repositories in the AWS account.
- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Check the response for the `imageScanningConfiguration` which should indicate if scanning is `scanOnPush` (set to `true`).
- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** If `imageScanningConfiguration` shows `"scanOnPush": true`.
  - **Fail:** If `imageScanningConfiguration` shows `"scanOnPush": false` or the property is not defined.

This approach aligns with current AWS capabilities and ensures that image vulnerability scanning settings within AWS ECR repositories are correctly configured. It helps maintain a secure cloud posture by mitigating the risk of deploying vulnerable container images.
